nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: 1  Scottish, English:  see  Moore  .

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Sir William Moore was born in 1403, in Otterburn, Yorkshire, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Thomasine Maleverer. He died in 1490, in Glemsford, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 87.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir William Moore est né en 1403, à Otterburn, Everwyck, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Thomasine Maleverer. Il est décédé en 1490, à Glemsford, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 87 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir William Moore wurde 1403 in Otterburn, Yorkshire, England geboren. Er hatte mit Thomasine Maleverer mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1490 im Alter von 87 Jahren in Glemsford, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Sir William Moore è nato il nel 1403 nel Otterburn, Yorkshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Thomasine Maleverer. Morì nel 1490, nel Glemsford, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 87 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Moore(은)는 1403년 영국 요르크실르 오터번에서 태어났다. 그는 Thomasine Maleverer(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 글레스퍼드에서 87세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir William Moore nasceu em 1403, em Otterburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Thomasine Maleverer. Ele faleceu em 1490, em Glemsford, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 87 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir William Moore родился в 1403 г. в местности оттирбурн, еркшир, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Thomasine Maleverer. Он умер в 1490 г. в местности глемсфорд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 87 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sir William Moore nació en 1403, en Otterburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Thomasine Maleverer. Murió en 1490, en Glemsford, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 87 años.
